prediction.summary: Multiple SSH brute-force attempts were observed targeting privileged accounts, specifically 'admin' and 'root', from various source IPs. A critical event occurred when the 'admin' account was successfully compromised from IP 192.168.1.50 after a series of failed password attempts. A session was subsequently opened for the 'admin' user, indicating unauthorized access.
recommendations: Immediately block the source IP 192.168.1.50 at the firewall or security group level.
Disable the 'admin' account and force a password reset. Revoke all active sessions for this user.
Conduct a thorough investigation of the 'admin' session from 192.168.1.50 to identify any post-login activity, such as command execution, data exfiltration, or lateral movement.
Review logs for any other successful logins from 192.168.1.50, 192.168.1.51, and 10.0.0.99, and investigate any associated activity.
Implement stronger authentication mechanisms, such as key-based authentication and Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), for all privileged accounts and SSH access.
Deploy or configure rate-limiting tools (e.g., fail2ban) to automatically block IPs exhibiting brute-force behavior.
